subject: Improve clock transition stability and get OSC1 freq from DT (#35)
author: Michael Huang

impact.effect_if_absent: CPU frequency scaling (cpufreq) subsystem would be non-functional; users cannot scale CPU frequency from 400 MHz to 1.2 GHz; no build or boot error would result—the feature silently fails to register

userspace_coupling.interface: standard /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu[01]/cpufreq/ sysfs (scaling_max_freq, scaling_cur_freq, cpuinfo_max_freq, etc.); consumers are community overclock scripts on /media/fat, not Main_MiSTer

license_provenance: GPL-2.0 (SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0 in socfpga-cpufreq.c); author: Michael Huang <coolbho3000@gmail.com>
notes: This commit (#35) is squashed together with commit #34 (Add cpufreq/overclock driver) in carried patch 0003. It improves the initial cpufreq driver by (1) removing the hardcoded 25 MHz OSC1 constant and reading it from the device tree via of_get_property() instead, and (2) adding PLL bypass/unbypass sequencing in set_dividers() for more stable clock transitions. The commit modifies calculate_vco_clock_hz() to use a static variable socfpga_cpufreq_osc1_hz instead of the hardcoded #define. At init time (socfpga_cpufreq_init()), it traverses /clkmgr@ffd04000/clocks/osc1 and reads the clock-frequency property from DT, converting it from big-endian. Verified against vanilla 6.18.38: no socfpga-cpufreq.c exists in vanilla (grep -rin 'socfpga|altera|altr' drivers/cpufreq/ returns no matches). Not upstream; never submitted to linux-pm. Forward-ported successfully from 5.15 to 6.18 as part of patch 0003. Known latent bug deliberately preserved for hardware safety: wait_for_fsm() calls wait_on_bit() with CLKMGR_STAT_BUSY (a mask, BIT(0)==1) where a bit *number* is expected, so it polls bit 1 instead of bit 0. This is intentional to avoid changing PLL transition timing without DE10-Nano hardware validation. In practice the bug is benign because PLL settling is covered by register-write latency. **CRITICAL: Silent failure mode.** Dropping this commit causes no build/boot error; cpufreq simply fails to register and users lose frequency-scaling capability. Userspace consumers: community overclock scripts on /media/fat that write to scaling_max_freq. Main_MiSTer itself does not reference cpufreq (verified in provenance-doc). Configuration: CONFIG_ARM_SOCFPGA_CPUFREQ=y in linux.config. Fully agrees with provenance-doc sections 719-874. sonnet-verified: corrected — OSC1-from-DT read (clkmgr@ffd04000/clocks/osc1, clock-frequency=<25000000>) confirmed against /mnt/source/linux/arch/arm/boot/dts/intel/socfpga/socfpga_cyclone5.dtsi:13-18; wait_on_bit(word,bit,mode) signature (bit is a bit NUMBER, not a mask) confirmed against /mnt/source/linux/include/linux/wait_bit.h, so the CLKMGR_STAT_BUSY=BIT(0) latent-bug claim holds. Two corrections made: (1) carried_mode changed clean-apply -> re-implemented — the carried patch 0003 hardens this commit's own osc1-DT-lookup hunk with NULL-checked goto error paths (out_unmap/out_put_clocks/out_put_osc1, pr_err diagnostic) that do not exist in the 5.15 original, which just chained of_get_child_by_name()/of_get_property()/be32_to_cpup() with no NULL checks and would have oopsed on a missing property — not a byte-identical carry. (2) userspace_coupling.coupled changed true -> false to match record 3d72b9db7's own record and the patch 0003 header itself ("Main_MiSTer does NOT read cpufreq (verified: no 'cpufreq' and no 'scaling_' anywhere in its sources)"); the prior coupled=true asserted userspace coupling with no Main_MiSTer path:line citation, an escalation-triggering violation per the grounding contract. Standard cpufreq sysfs is real but its only consumers are external community overclock scripts, not Main_MiSTer or any cited userspace path.
